rationale.claude: Strong scope coverage and includes the AI-pipeline-as-evidence-of-initiative angle well. Held back from target tier by two recurring patterns: (1) the paragraphs are walls (3 long dense paragraphs with no breathing room between substantive moves), and (2) several AI-tell soft phrases drift in — 'I thrive on ensuring seamless operations', 'commitment to critical thinking and driving continuous improvement, not just following existing playbooks', 'strong awareness of security and confidentiality standards from extensive HIPAA training' (the 'extensive' qualifier is inflated for combat-medic patient-records work). These aren't lies but they're the kind of LLM-flavor language that a reader's pattern-detector catches as 'this was written by an AI.' The substance behind each AI-tell is real; rephrasing in the candidate's own voice would close the gap.

key_strengths: Specific hook tied to TopDog Law's 'building something meaningful and making a visible impact'
Concrete BTB scope (Tier 1-2 support, hardware/OS/network across live operations, ticketing, RustDesk)
AI Job-Application Pipeline used as evidence-of-pattern rather than self-promotion ('the same habit I bring to support work: spotting repeated friction, documenting it, and improving the workflow')
Closes with specific action ('contribute my technical support expertise and dedication to operational excellence to TopDog Law')
key_weaknesses: 'I thrive on ensuring seamless operations' — AI-tell phrasing; no specific candidate experience drives the claim
'extensive HIPAA training' overclaims — Carlos has combat-medic patient-records experience, not formal HIPAA training programs
'commitment to critical thinking and driving continuous improvement, not just following existing playbooks' reads as a values-statement template
3 paragraphs each running 4-5 sentences without breaks — dense walls reduce scannability
Mentions 'security and confidentiality standards' without naming a specific behavior — what does the reader learn?
specific_fixes: Rewrite 'I thrive on ensuring seamless operations' as a concrete observation ('I keep ticket queues moving and document fixes once' or similar)
Replace 'extensive HIPAA training' with 'medical records handling discipline from Army Reserve service' — true, scoped, doesn't claim formal training
Cut 'commitment to critical thinking and driving continuous improvement, not just following existing playbooks' — replace with a one-sentence concrete example
Break the 3 walls into 4-5 shorter paragraphs with one move per paragraph
Replace 'security and confidentiality standards' with a concrete behavior (e.g. 'I default to least-privilege when granting access')
tier_definition_match: Matches nearmiss (~8): all structural moves are right (hook, scope coverage, project-as-evidence, specific closing) and the substance is correct. What's blocking 9+ is the soft-phrase drift — recognizable AI-tells that a careful editor would rephrase in 5 minutes without changing what the letter says. Distinction vs target: target avoids the soft phrases in the first draft, nearmiss has them surviving into the final.
